name: First Southeast Bank
description_short: Community bank in Harmony, Minnesota offering personal checking, savings, loans, and agricultural/business banking with online and mobile services since 1893.
description_long:
First Southeast Bank traces its roots to 1893 when the Bank of Harmony was founded by Tollef Sanderson. The institution was formally incorporated and licensed in 1904 as Harmony State Bank, operating continuously through the Great Depression with FDIC membership granted in 1932. The bank consolidated with 1st National Bank in 1956 and has undergone multiple renovations and ownership changes, most recently becoming First Southeast Bank. Today it operates from its Harmony, Minnesota location with a focus on personalized community banking.

The bank offers a comprehensive suite of retail and business banking services. Personal banking includes checking accounts, savings products, and consumer loans (auto, home, recreational vehicle financing). Agricultural and business banking serves farm operations and small businesses. Digital services include online banking, mobile deposit functionality, online bill pay, and electronic banking platforms. The website prominently features financial calculators and educational resources on security and fraud prevention.

First Southeast Bank distinguishes itself through its emphasis on personal service and long community history spanning 130+ years. The bank maintains a physical branch location with staff-based service and has invested in modernization including early adoption of computer systems (1984) and mobile banking technology. Their routing number (091204462) indicates Federal Reserve participation and full banking infrastructure.

As a single-location or small regional bank, First Southeast Bank primarily serves customers in the Harmony, Minnesota area and those with ties to agricultural/business communities in that region. The website provides limited transparency regarding interest rates, account fees, loan terms, or competitive product details. Customers seeking national branch networks, high-yield savings rates, or extensive product variety may find better options elsewhere. The bank's local focus and personal touch appeal to community-oriented customers but limit accessibility for remote or nationwide users. Many banks offer installment loans with fixed monthly payments, giving borrowers a clear payoff timeline.
services: Consumer loans (auto, home, tractor, recreational vehicle)
In-person banking at Harmony, Minnesota location
pros: 130+ year operating history with continuous FDIC insurance coverage since 1932, demonstrating stability and regulatory compliance
Mobile deposit and online bill pay available, enabling digital-first banking for customers
Offers agricultural and business banking services, addressing niche market needs often underserved by national banks
Personal touch and community-focused service model with local decision-making
Comprehensive loan products covering auto, home, tractor, and recreational vehicle financing
Early technology adopter with in-house computer systems since 1984 and modern eBanking platform
Consumer fraud education resources (check washing scams, FTC alerts) prominently featured on website
cons: Single physical location in Harmony, Minnesota with no branch network, limiting accessibility for non-local customers
Website provides no information on interest rates, APYs, loan terms, or fee schedules, making competitive comparison impossible
Limited online presence and marketing—no social media links, mobile app information, or detailed product documentation visible
No information on minimum balance requirements, overdraft policies, or account features compared to regional competitors
Appears to be a legacy institution with minimal digital marketing presence, potentially making it difficult for younger/digital-first consumers to engage
best_for: Farmers and agricultural business owners in southeastern Minnesota seeking specialized ag lending and local relationship banking
Long-time Harmony, Minnesota residents and community members with established local ties and preference for personalized service
Small business owners in the region needing flexible lending and local banking relationships rather than national bank automation
Customers prioritizing bank stability and 130+ year track record over high-yield rates or extensive digital features
diagnosis: First Southeast Bank is best for community-based customers in the Harmony, Minnesota area, particularly farmers, small business owners, and long-time residents seeking personalized service and local lending relationships. The primary caveat is geographic limitation and lack of digital transparency—customers outside the region or those comparing rates/fees will find the website insufficient, and the bank's single-location model makes it inaccessible for those requiring multi-state banking convenience.
typical_results_timeline: Account opening typically takes 1-3 business days. Loan decisions vary by product.
